name: Chadar Trek (Frozen River)
description: Walk on the frozen Zanskar River for 62km over 9 days at -25°C to -35°C. One of the most extreme treks in India, requiring ice walking, canyon camping, and absolute cold tolerance.
warnings: River ice can crack without warning

name: Pin Parvati Pass Trek
description: Cross from Parvati Valley to Spiti Valley over the 5319m Pin Parvati Pass. A serious high-altitude trek with glacier crossings, river fords, and remote terrain with no rescue access.
warnings: River crossings can be dangerous after rain

name: Hampta Pass Trek
description: Cross from the lush Kullu Valley to the barren Lahaul Valley over Hampta Pass at 4270m. Dramatic landscape transition from green meadows to stark desert in just 4 days.
highlights: Views of Deo Tibba and Indrasan
warnings: Stream crossings can swell after rain
Snow patches on pass even in summer

name: Triund Trek
description: A popular day trek from McLeodganj to the Triund ridge at 2875m with panoramic views of the Dhauladhar range and Kangra Valley. Easy gradient, well-marked trail.
warnings: Very crowded on weekends and holidays
No water sources on trail — carry your own
Trail can be slippery in monsoon

name: Kheerganga Trek
description: Trek through Parvati Valley forests to natural hot springs at 2960m. The trail passes through dense forests, waterfalls, and the sacred hot water pool at the top.
warnings: Trail narrow and exposed in sections
Hot springs area gets very crowded

name: Chandrashila Summit Trek
description: Summit Chandrashila peak at 4000m via the highest Shiva temple at Tungnath (3680m). Panoramic views of Nanda Devi, Trisul, Kedar, Bandarpunch, and Chaukhamba peaks.
highlights: Nanda Devi visible on clear days
warnings: Ice on trail in winter — microspikes needed
Altitude gain is rapid from Chopta
No shelter between Tungnath and summit

name: Valley of Flowers Trek
description: UNESCO World Heritage Site with 600+ species of wildflowers blooming July-September. Combined with Hemkund Sahib visit. The valley is closed outside monsoon season.
warnings: Permits required from forest office in Ghangaria

name: Roopkund Trek (Mystery Lake)
description: Trek to the glacial mystery lake at 4800m containing 500+ human skeletons from 9th century. Passes through Ali and Bedni Bugyal — Asia's largest alpine meadows.
warnings: Roopkund area frequently restricted by forest dept
Heavy snow possible even in May

name: Kedarkantha Trek
description: India's most popular winter trek in Uttarkashi district. Summit at 3810m with 360° views of Swargarohini, Bandarpunch, Black Peak, and Kedarnath ranges. Deep snow December-March.
highlights: Best winter snow trek in India
warnings: Sankri base is 10 hours from Dehradun

name: Kuari Pass Trek (Lord Curzon Trail)
description: Follow the trail Lord Curzon walked in 1905 through dense oak and rhododendron forests to Kuari Pass at 3876m. Unbroken views of Nanda Devi, Dronagiri, Kamet, and Mana peaks.
warnings: Heavy snow in winter makes trail hard to find
No phone network for most of trek
Steep descent from pass can be tough on knees
Road to Joshimath base prone to landslides

name: Rupin Pass Trek
description: Cross from Uttarakhand to Himachal Pradesh over the 4650m Rupin Pass. Features hanging villages, dramatic waterfalls, snow bridges, and one of India's most photogenic passes.
warnings: 7 days with no exit — must be committed

name: Goechala Trek
description: Face-to-face with Kanchenjunga (8586m) from the Goechala viewpoint in Sikkim at 4940m. Passes through Kanchenjunga National Park with pristine forests and glacial lakes.
warnings: Very remote — no evacuation by helicopter easily
Must carry all supplies from Yuksom

name: Brahmatal Trek
description: A beautiful winter trek to the frozen Brahmatal lake at 3450m with views of Mt Trisul and Nanda Ghunti. Less crowded alternative to Kedarkantha with equally stunning snow.
highlights: Mt Trisul and Nanda Ghunti views
warnings: Trail through deep snow in Jan-Feb
Lohajung base is a long drive from Kathgodam

name: Ali Bedni Bugyal Trek
description: Trek to Asia's largest high-altitude meadows — Ali Bugyal and Bedni Bugyal. Rolling green pastures at 3500m with a wall of Himalayan peaks as backdrop.
highlights: Ali Bugyal — Asia's largest alpine meadow
warnings: No shelter on open meadows — weather exposure risk
Trail from Wan is steep initially

name: Deoria Tal - Chandrashila Trek
description: Combine the reflective Deoria Tal lake with Tungnath temple and Chandrashila summit. Start at Sari village, camp at Deoria Tal, traverse to Chopta, summit Chandrashila at 4000m.
warnings: Long traverse day from Deoria Tal to Chopta
Trail through forest can be confusing
Rapid altitude gain on summit day

name: Beas Kund Trek
description: Short trek to the glacial source of the Beas River at 3700m near Manali. Easy gradient through meadows with views of Friendship Peak, Shitidhar, and Ladakhi peaks.
warnings: Boulder field near Beas Kund — ankle injury risk
Snow near lake even in June
Solang Valley road crowded on weekends

name: Bhrigu Lake Trek
description: Trek to the sacred Bhrigu Lake at 4300m near Manali — one of the highest lakes accessible in a short trek. Alpine meadows, snow fields, and a glacial lake with Himalayan backdrop.
highlights: Views of Pir Panjal and Dhauladhar ranges
warnings: Rapid altitude gain — 1400m in one day
AMS risk due to fast ascent
Very steep final section to lake

name: Khaliya Top Trek
description: Trek to the Khaliya Top meadow at 3500m above Munsiyari for close-up views of the Panchachuli peaks. A short but rewarding trek through rhododendron and oak forests.
warnings: Munsiyari is a long drive from Kathgodam (10 hours)

name: Sar Pass Trek
description: Cross the Sar Pass at 4200m from Kasol side through dense forests, snow fields, and alpine meadows. Known for its fun snow sliding section on the descent from the pass.
warnings: Heavy snow on pass in early season
Trail through forest can be disorienting in fog

name: Great Himalayan Trail (GHT India Section)
description: Multi-section traverse across the Indian Himalaya — the ultimate long-distance trek. Crosses multiple high passes, valleys, and climate zones. Only for experienced trekkers with expedition-level fitness.
highlights: Remote valleys with no tourist infrastructure
warnings: Sections with no trail and no people for days
Multiple river crossings and glacier traverses
No rescue infrastructure on most sections

name: Nag Tibba Trek
description: The highest peak in the lower Himalayas of Uttarakhand at 3022m. A perfect beginner trek near Mussoorie with views of Bandarpunch, Swargarohini, and Kedarnath peaks.
highlights: Highest peak in lower Garhwal Himalayas
warnings: Cold at summit camp in winter

name: Tarsar Marsar Trek
description: Trek to the twin alpine lakes of Tarsar and Marsar in Kashmir. Turquoise Tarsar and dark green Marsar sit in high mountain bowls surrounded by peaks. Among Kashmir's finest treks.
warnings: Weather changes rapidly in Kashmir highlands
Security situation — check advisories before travel

name: Kashmir Great Lakes Trek
description: Visit 7 stunning alpine lakes in 7 days across the Kashmir highlands. Vishansar, Krishansar, Gadsar, Satsar, Gangabal — each lake a different colour in a different setting.
warnings: No phone coverage for entire trek
Weather unpredictable — rain/snow possible any day

name: Kolahoi Glacier Trek
description: Trek to the snout of the Kolahoi Glacier — Kashmir's largest glacier and the source of the Lidder River. Remote approach through pine forests and boulder fields to the glacier face.
highlights: Aru Valley approach through pine forests
warnings: Boulder field approach to glacier is tough
Trail poorly marked beyond tree line

name: Milam Glacier Trek
description: Trek along the old Indo-Tibet trade route to the Milam Glacier at 4500m in the Kumaon Himalayas. Passes through abandoned villages, gorges, and stunning high-altitude terrain near the Tibet border.
warnings: Inner line permit required — apply in advance
Very remote — no phone or medical access
Only feasible pre and post monsoon
